ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for martabak manis (Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake). FOODON_03301710 (pancake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pancakes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for martabak manis with cheese and milk filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake. SNOMED CT does not cover this food at the required specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of 3 fun-sized pieces of cheesy milk martabak, weighing approximately 150 g in total, as described by the vendor.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Manis (Sweet Martabak) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central pancake analogue and Indonesian food composition data (FAO/INFOODS SEA) with cheese and milk filling adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) and FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for martabak manis keju susu exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for plain sweet pancakes (USDA FDC NDB 18259, 'Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe') with adjustments for cheese and sweetened condensed milk filling typical of martabak manis, cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA composition data for Indonesian sweet pancakes. Per-100g values reflect the composite food as typically prepared. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Pancakes, made from mix — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; adapted for martabak manis with cheese and milk filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis keju susu. The GI of 67 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweet pancakes/pancakes made from mix (GI ~67, medium category). Martabak manis shares a similar batter composition (wheat flour, eggs, sugar, milk) and cooking method (pan-fried). The cheese and milk filling adds fat and protein, which may moderately lower the effective GI, but 67 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (67 × 40.0 g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.5 serving factor) / 100 = 40.2 g GL for 150g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~280 mg/100g; ~420 mg per 150g serving) from cheese and batter ingredients
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one piece and avoid additional salty toppings, as a full 3-piece serving contributes approximately 420 mg sodium, representing about 18% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content (~40g carbohydrate and ~18g sugar per 100g) with medium GI of 67
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat martabak manis keju susu as an occasional indulgence only; a 150g serving delivers approximately 60g of carbohydrate and 27g of sugar, producing a high glycemic load (~40), which will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and requires careful insulin or medication adjustment.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6g/100g; ~9g per serving) and cholesterol (~75mg/100g) from cheese, butter, and eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of martabak manis keju susu due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content; a 150g serving provides approximately 9g of saturated fat, which is close to the recommended daily limit of 10–13g for cardiovascular risk reduction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheesy Milk Martabak Original (3 pieces) is a fun-sized serving of martabak manis, the beloved Indonesian sweet stuffed thick pancake, filled with a classic combination of processed cheese and sweetened condensed milk. Martabak manis — also known as terang bulan in some regions of Indonesia — is made from a leavened batter of wheat flour, eggs, sugar, milk, butter, baking powder, and yeast, which is poured into a heavy cast-iron mould and cooked until the base is golden and the interior is soft, spongy, and honeycomb-textured. The filling of cheese and milk is spread over the hot pancake, which is then folded and sliced into pieces. The result is a rich, sweet, slightly salty, and indulgent dessert with a tender crumb and a buttery, caramelised crust. This 3-piece fun-sized variant, served by Smile Martabak at Funan in Singapore, is designed as an individual portion weighing approximately 150g. Nutritionally, each 150g serving provides approximately 435 kcal, 60g of carbohydrate, 27g of sugar, 16.5g of fat, and 9g of saturated fat, making it a high-energy, high-carbohydrate dessert. The medium glycemic index (~67) and high glycemic load (~40 per serving) make it unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The sodium content (~420mg per serving) is also relevant for those managing hypertension. Martabak manis is a culturally significant street food in Indonesia, commonly sold by roadside vendors and now available in modern food courts and delivery platforms across Southeast Asia, including Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for martabak manis keju susu exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy pancake data and FAO/INFOODS SEA Indonesian food composition data, adjusted for the cheese and milk filling. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous food (sweet pancakes). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Beverages' (as in input) to 'Desserts': martabak manis is a sweet stuffed pancake, not a beverage. The input food_group appears to be a data entry error from the field survey seed record. 2. Serving size of 150g (3 pieces) is preserved from the input description. 3. Nutrient values are estimated; a direct laboratory analysis or an approved primary source entry for martabak manis keju susu would improve confidence to high. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source composition for this food type. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source composition for this food type. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source composition for this food type. 7. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (sweet pancakes/pancakes made from mix); no direct GI measurement for martabak manis keju susu was found in any approved GI source. 8. Glycemic load per serving = (67 × 40.0 / 100) × 1.5 = 40.2 for a 150g serving. 9. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (Smile Martabak, Indonesian cuisine); formal halal certification was not verified from an approved source. 10. The food_name in the input ('Cheesy Milk Original (3pcs)') has been expanded to 'Cheesy Milk Martabak Original (3 pieces)' for canonical clarity.
